sentence: Subjective:
HPI: Miss Ross is a 25-year-old female presenting with symptoms of discomfort, change in discharge, and odor in her vaginal region.
She reports that the right side of her vagina has become painful.
She denies any fevers or chills.
She recently learned that her partner has been in sexual encounters with other people, prompting her to seek evaluation for all STDs.
Past medical history: The patient has a past abnormal pap smear at age 20, but has not had a pap smear since then.
She is currently 25 years old.
Review of systems: [NOT MENTIONED]
Current medications: [NOT MENTIONED]
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: The pelvic exam demonstrates no external lesions, normal labia majora and minora, normal physiological discharge with normal color and smell.
The cervix is normal, vaginal wall is normal, no cervical motion tenderness, and no adnexal tenderness or masses noted.
Test Results: [NOT MENTIONED]
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient is at high risk for STDs due to an abnormal past pap smear and her partner's history of multiple sexual partners.
Problem list: STD risk; abnormal past pap smear; high risk for STDs.
Plan:
I prescribed [ NOT MENTIONED ]
I ordered STD testing for gonorrhea, chlamydia, trichomonas, and blood tests for syphilis, HIV, and hepatitis C.
I instructed the patient to call the office in 3 days for results, or earlier if she receives a call from the office.
Follow-up: The patient will follow up in 3 days for results, or at her next annual checkup if all results are normal.
